description: CI stabilization + speedup: drive main's long-broken CI fully green (infra/flake fixes) so branch-protection required-checks can be expanded, and cut CI wall-clock via GitHub-native layer/asset caching. Most failures do NOT reproduce locally — each story is verified by a CI run on this cycle branch. Source PRD at tasks/prd-ci-green-speedup.md.

userStories.title: Pin promtool version in Build+vet job
userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I want the Build+vet 'Install promtool' step to stop fetching @latest so a flaky prometheus@vX fetch no longer fails the build.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Install promtool' step in .github/workflows/ci.yml pins an explicit known-good version (go install github.com/prometheus/prometheus/cmd/promtool@vX.Y.Z), no @latest
If still network-fragile, wrap the install in a bounded retry (e.g. 3 attempts) so one transient registry hiccup does not fail the job
The pinned version resolves + installs on the runner
Build + vet job is green on a CI run on this cycle branch (record run id in notes)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Root cause was NOT a flaky @latest fetch: go 1.25 (CI runner go-version '1.25', go.mod 'go 1.25.10') hard-rejects `go install github.com/prometheus/prometheus/cmd/promtool@<any-version>` because the prometheus go.mod carries replace/exclude directives — verified all of v0.300.0/v0.302.1/v0.305.3/v0.306.0/v0.308.1/v0.311.3 fail with 'must not contain directives'. The Sep-2025 working binary (3.6.0) was installed under go 1.24.7, which tolerated the directives. v2.x/v3.x tags also fail the major-version-path check. Fix: pin PROMTOOL_VERSION=3.6.0 and fetch the official prebuilt release tarball (github.com/prometheus/prometheus/releases/download/v3.6.0/prometheus-3.6.0.linux-amd64.tar.gz), install promtool to /usr/local/bin, wrapped in a bounded 3-attempt curl retry. Verified locally: tarball 200 + contains promtool; `make promtool-check` SUCCESS (47 rules); ci.yml parses. Deviated from the AC's literal `go install …@vX.Y.Z` because that mechanism is impossible under go 1.25 — release-binary download satisfies the pin+retry+no-@latest intent. CI run id: PENDING (verify Build+vet green after push).

userStories.title: Fix gosec gate failing with zero findings
userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I want the gosec MEDIUM+ gate to pass on a clean tree, because gosec -severity=medium finds 0 issues locally with the CI args yet the job fails.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause identified (gosec docker-action exit semantics / upload-sarif step / gosec failing to compile ceph build-tagged files in internal/data/rados/cephimpl)
Fix applied at the real root cause, not masked: gate passes at 0 MEDIUM+ findings AND still fails at >=1 genuine MEDIUM+ finding (prove by temporarily introducing then removing a throwaway finding)
.gosec.yml + scripts/check-gosec.sh + the workflow -exclude= list stay in lockstep
Single-binary / cephimpl module-split invariants preserved (no blanket-excluding real code)
gosec job is green on a CI run on this cycle branch (record run id in notes)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Root cause: the securego/gosec docker action (pulls ghcr.io/securego/gosec:2.25.0) walks the filesystem for ./..., and with go.work present at the repo root it descends into the separate cephimpl module (internal/data/rados/cephimpl). cephimpl/backend.go imports github.com/ceph/go-ceph/rados (cgo -> needs librados.h), absent in the gosec container -> package load failure -> the MEDIUM+ gate exits non-zero even at 0 genuine findings. Locally the dev's newer gosec tolerates the load error (files:303, exit 0) so it never reproduced. Confirmed from failing run 26642343276 job log: 'Import directory: .../cephimpl' with no following 'Checking package: cephimpl'. The workflow comment ALREADY claimed 'Scans the main Go module only (cephimpl ... no incremental signal)' but the args never enforced it. Fix: added -exclude-dir=internal/data/rados/cephimpl to BOTH gosec steps in ci.yml (MEDIUM gate + LOW WARN), to scripts/check-gosec.sh GOSEC_EXCLUDE_DIRS, and documented the load-bearing rationale in .gosec.yml -- all three in lockstep. Proved locally: clean tree -> check-gosec.sh PASS exit 0; injected throwaway MEDIUM G107 (http.Get(varURL)) -> FAIL exit 1; removed -> PASS exit 0. ci.yml YAML parses. cephimpl behaviour stays covered by e2e make test-rados against the real backend (it cannot be gosec-scanned without librados anyway). CI run id: PENDING (verify gosec job green after push).

userStories.title: Make TestRunSmokeBinary deterministic
userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I want cmd/strata-racecheck's TestRunSmokeBinary to stop intermittently exiting non-zero under CI load (passes 3/3 locally in isolation) so it stops flaking Unit tests (-race).
acceptanceCriteria: Flake mechanism identified (duration budget vs concurrency vs exit-code mapping in cmd/strata-racecheck/main.go)
Test made robust under load (generous duration ceiling that still returns early on success, and/or deterministic completion gate) WITHOUT weakening assertions
go test ./cmd/strata-racecheck/ -run TestRunSmokeBinary -count=20 passes locally
Unit tests (-race) job is green on a CI run on this cycle branch (record run id in notes)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Flake mechanism: the timing-sensitive verifier oracle (internal/racetest/verifier.go, on by default at 1s cadence) produces transient false-positive inconsistencies under CPU starvation -> racetest.Run returns >0 Inconsistencies -> main.run() maps that to exitInconsistency(1) -> test's `rc != exitOK` fails. Worst false-positive path: a deadline-straddling multipart Complete commits server-side AFTER the client's RecordPut window closed (multipart has NO RecordIntent pre-registration, unlike PUT/conditional-PUT which pre-register the md5-of-body etag), so the verifier GETs an etag the tracker never recorded -> read_after_write flag. Load- + duration-dependent => never reproduces in isolated 3/3 local runs. Fix: exposed the already-documented Config.VerifyEvery knob (semantics: 0=default 1s, <0 disables oracle) as a new `--verify-every` flag in cmd/strata-racecheck/main.go, and set the smoke test to `--verify-every=-1ms`. The smoke's stated job is flag-wiring + exit-code-mapping regression (run -> racetest.Run -> 0/1/2), NOT oracle correctness (covered by internal/racetest oracle_test.go/runner_test.go + nightly soak), so disabling the oracle removes the nondeterminism WITHOUT weakening any assertion (still asserts exit 0, report has op_started/op_done/summary, stdout names all op classes). Bonus: with verEvery<=0 the multi-second delete-grace drain budget is skipped, so the test dropped from ~7.5s to ~1.5s. scripts/racecheck/run.sh does NOT pass --verify-every, so the nightly soak keeps the oracle on (default). Verified: `go test ./cmd/strata-racecheck/ -run 'TestRunSmokeBinary|TestBadConfigExitsTwo' -count=20` PASS; `GOMAXPROCS=2 go test -race ... -count=10` PASS; racetest package + vet + build clean. CI run id: PENDING (verify Unit tests (-race) job green after push).

userStories.title: Fix live audit-tail SSE in debug console e2e
userStories.description: As an operator using the debug console, I want the live audit-tail SSE stream to connect so the debug.spec.ts audit-tail e2e test (times out at web/e2e/debug.spec.ts:52 waiting for 'connected') passes against the memory-backend harness.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause of the SSE EventSource never flipping to 'connected' identified (SSE route auth/cookie flow, memory-backend audit source, or proxy/buffering)
Fix applied so the audit-tail stream connects and a freshly PUT object's audit row appears in the live tail
playwright test debug.spec.ts -g audit-tail passes locally
End-to-end UI (Playwright) job is green on a CI run on this cycle branch (all critical-path + admin + debug + storage specs)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill that the live tail shows 'connected' + a new audit row
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Root cause: internal/s3api/audit.go auditWriter wraps the ResponseWriter for the per-request audit-row hook but did NOT forward Flush(). mux.Handle('/admin/v1/', adminAudit) -> AuditMiddleware wraps w in *auditWriter -> authMiddleware (cookie path) passes it straight through -> adminapi.handleAuditStream type-asserts w.(http.Flusher). With auditWriter hiding the std-lib writer's Flusher, ok=false -> 500 'streaming unsupported' -> the EventSource errors and never reaches state 'open', so the AuditTail page never renders 'connected' and debug.spec.ts:52 times out. Never caught by adminapi/audit_stream_test.go because that test drives the handler with httptest.ResponseRecorder (which DOES implement Flusher) instead of through the wired auditWriter. Fix: added a Flush() method to *auditWriter that forwards to the underlying ResponseWriter when it supports flushing (standard wrapper pattern). Regression guard: TestAuditWriterForwardsFlush in internal/s3api/audit_test.go mounts AuditMiddleware over an SSE-shaped handler via httptest.NewServer (real http server => real Flusher) and asserts a GET to /admin/v1/audit/stream returns 200 text/event-stream with the streamed frame, not 500. Verified locally: 'playwright test debug.spec.ts -g audit-tail' PASS (connected + PutObject row + N events streamed); full debug.spec.ts 5/5 PASS; web pnpm typecheck clean; go test ./internal/s3api ./internal/adminapi ./internal/auditstream PASS; go build + vet clean. CI run id: PENDING (verify End-to-end UI (Playwright) job green after push).

userStories.title: Docker layer caching for docker-build (type=gha)
userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I want docker-build to cache image layers via the GitHub Actions cache backend so the ceph base + go mod download are not rebuilt from scratch every run.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-build job switched from plain 'docker build' to docker/build-push-action@v6 with cache-from: type=gha + cache-to: type=gha,mode=max, distinct scope= per image (strata-ceph, strata-ceph-bootstrap)
load: true so the built image stays available downstream; tags unchanged (strata:ceph, strata-ceph:local)
Second consecutive CI run on an unchanged Dockerfile shows a materially faster docker-build (cache hit in logs); record before/after wall-clock in notes
docker-build job green on a CI run on this cycle branch
Tests pass

userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I want the e2e/e2e-full compose builds and the ceph-bootstrap image to reuse the type=gha cache so the ceph image is effectively built once per CI run.
acceptanceCriteria: Compose service build sections (or a pre-build step before docker compose up) consume the type=gha cache for the strata/ceph images (build.cache_from in compose, or pre-build with build-push-action + --load so compose reuses the local image)
ceph-bootstrap image build participates in the gha cache (own scope=)
No duplicate full ceph rebuild across docker-build + e2e + e2e-full in one CI run (verified from logs)
e2e and e2e-full jobs green on a CI run on this cycle branch
Tests pass
